title: Junior Consultant Rheumatologist - Private Healthcare Facility
description_text:
SearchPlus HR is excited to offer an opportunity for a Junior Consultant Rheumatologist to join a prestigious private healthcare facility. This is an excellent role for a motivated and passionate medical professional looking to develop their expertise in rheumatology while making a difference in patient care.
As a Junior Consultant Rheumatologist, you will work closely under the mentorship of experienced rheumatologists, engaging in the evaluation and management of patients with various rheumatic conditions. Your role will involve patient assessments, diagnostic testing, and contributing to treatment plans, all while receiving valuable guidance to enhance your clinical skills.
Key Responsibilities:
Assist in conducting thorough patient evaluations and compiling medical histories.
Diagnose and manage patients with rheumatic diseases, under the supervision of senior consultants.
Order and interpret necessary diagnostic tests, including blood tests and imaging.
Contribute to developing and implementing treatment plans in collaboration with the healthcare team.
Educate patients and their families on disease management and treatment options.
Participate in team meetings and case discussions to enhance collaborative care.
Engage in professional development opportunities, such as mentoring and educational workshops.
Maintain accurate documentation of patient records and treatment progress.
Completion of residency in Internal Medicine.
Strong interest in the field of rheumatology and a commitment to patient care.
Good clinical and diagnostic skills.
Effective communication and interpersonal skills to interact with patients and team members.
A positive attitude and enthusiasm for learning and professional development.
Ability to work collaboratively within a multidisciplinary team environment.
Detail-oriented and organized, with strong time management abilities.
Willingness to engage in continued education and stay updated on advancements in rheumatology.
Working Time is Full Time.
Benefits are Good Monthly Salary + Incentives + Standard family benefits like accommodation, Air ticket, Medical insurance, and Education allowance
ai_core_responsibilities: As a Junior Consultant Rheumatologist, you will assist in patient evaluations, diagnostic testing, and treatment planning under the supervision of senior consultants. You will also engage in professional development and maintain accurate patient records.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must have completed a residency in Internal Medicine and possess a strong interest in rheumatology. Good clinical skills, effective communication, and a commitment to patient care are essential.

title: Senior Consultant Oncologist- Leading Healthcare Facility
description_text:
SearchPlus HR is proud to present an exciting opportunity for a Senior Consultant Oncologist at a leading healthcare facility. In this prestigious role, you will play a critical part in delivering high-quality cancer care and treatment to patients diagnosed with various forms of cancer.
As a Senior Consultant Oncologist, your expertise will guide clinical decision-making and treatment development in a collaborative environment. You will be responsible for leading patient evaluations, developing innovative treatment protocols, and mentoring junior oncologists and healthcare professionals within our team.
Key Responsibilities:
Conduct in-depth assessments to diagnose and evaluate the severity of cancer in patients, considering all relevant medical history and symptoms.
Order, interpret, and analyze diagnostic tests, including imaging studies and biopsies, to inform and guide treatment plans.
Design comprehensive, individualized treatment strategies using the latest advancements in oncology, including chemotherapy, immunotherapy, and targeted therapies.
Oversee and manage treatment protocols, ensuring effective monitoring of patient progress and proactive management of side effects.
Provide expert guidance and support to patients and their families, offering education about treatment options, supportive care resources, and survivorship planning.
Collaborate with cross-functional healthcare teams, including radiologists, surgeons, and nurses, to deliver coordinated and holistic patient care.
Engage in clinical research initiatives and contribute to the development of new oncological treatments and best practices.
Mentor and educate junior colleagues, fostering a culture of continuous learning and professional development within the department.
Proven expertise in managing complex cancer cases with a commitment to the highest standards of patient care.
Strong understanding of the latest treatment modalities and clinical trials in oncology.
Exceptional communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to connect with patients and colleagues alike.
A collaborative mindset, capable of working within multidisciplinary healthcare teams effectively.
Demonstrated leadership abilities and a passion for mentoring and developing junior medical staff.
Interest and experience in clinical research and academia are highly beneficial.
Commitment to continuous education and staying current with advances in oncology.
Private Health Insurance
Training & Development
Performance Bonus
ai_core_responsibilities: As a Senior Consultant Oncologist, you will conduct patient evaluations and develop innovative treatment protocols. You will also mentor junior oncologists and collaborate with healthcare teams to provide high-quality cancer care.
ai_requirements_summary: The role requires proven expertise in managing complex cancer cases and a strong understanding of the latest treatment modalities. Exceptional communication skills and a commitment to continuous education in oncology are also essential.
linkedin_org_locations: Office 113, Al Khaimah Building, Port Saeed Deira, Dubai UAE Tel:  00971 4 2555465, Deira, Dubai 25570, AE

title: Accounting Specialist
locations_alt_raw: Chapel Hill, North Carolina, United States
locations_derived: Chapel Hill, North Carolina, United States
description_text:
Are you an analytical and dependable individual who thrives in a fast-paced, flexible environment? Do you take pride in solving problems, ensuring accuracy, and keeping everything on track? If you're a natural multitasker and a self-starter who excels in a structured setting, this role is for you.
As a member of our accounting team, you will play a vital role in enhancing the efficiency and effectiveness of the accounting function within a fast-paced automotive environment. You’ll be involved in Accounts Payable (A/P), Accounts Receivable (A/R), and Payroll Processing, using Microsoft suite, QuickBooks, and Excel (including Pivot Tables and formulas). Your problem-solving skills, strong organizational abilities, and financial expertise will be crucial to maintaining accuracy and timeliness. 
This role is designed for someone who balances independence with collaboration and precision with perspective. We’re looking for a professional who values confidentiality, lives our company values, and is committed to continuous learning. Strong judgment is essential: the ability to identify errors, understand their broader financial impact, and focus effort where it creates meaningful results.
What you will do!
Accounting Support: Perform accurate and timely A/P, A/R, GL entries, reconciliations, and vendor account management. Monitor daily bank activity and ensure accuracy in all accounting transactions as well as biweekly Payroll processes.
Discrepancy Resolution: Research and resolve discrepancies; respond to internal accounting-related inquiries 
Journal Entries and Financial Reporting: Manage sales tax and tire disposal reporting requirements. Prepare journal entries and assist with monthly reconciliations, financial reports, budgets, and forecasts (GAAP-compliant).
Cross-Department Collaboration: Collaborate with teams across the organization to support overall business objectives and financial needs.
System & Process Improvement: Recommend and support the implementation of process improvements and administrative tools, while also assisting in the communication and integration of new administrative products or systems throughout the company.
Why Chapel Hill Tire?
This is more than a job, it’s a launchpad to build a career you’re proud of! When you’re ready to think beyond the day-to-day, you’ll find we’re just as committed to helping you build a strong, stable future - both personally and professionally.
We believe clear communication and collaboration are key to building strong relationships across our teams. Because we think a job should lift you up, not wear you down! Here, you get paid training, real career growth, a supportive team, and a work life balance!
In addition to reviewing resumes, qualifications, experience, we utilize the Culture Index, a powerful tool that helps us understand how to best connect and work with one another & identify overall fit. (You will receive a link to your email after submitting your application)
The requirements listed below are representative of the knowledge skill and/or ability required. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.
Education/Experience: High School Diploma or GED required. Bachelor's Degree in Accounting or related field, or practical experience preferred. Basic math and knowledge of business principles. 
Language Skills: Strong leadership, communication, organization, time management, and listening skills. 
Computer Skills: Proficient with QuickBooks (required!) and familiar/intermediate ability with Office 365 (Excel (Pivot tables & Formulas), PowerBI, Teams, SharePoint). Ability to navigate websites easily. 
Reasoning & Problem-Solving: Ability to use logic to identify solutions, collaborate effectively, and apply common sense to tasks. Strong customer service orientation and adaptability to prioritize diverse needs.
· Unlimited paid training & certifications
· No nights, weekends, or holidays (We are Mon-Fri 8-5!)
· Paid Time Off & paid holidays & bereavement leave
· Health, Dental, Vision, ST & LT Disability, FSA & more elective benefits to choose!
· 401K company match & profit sharing
· Company-paid life insurance & paid parental leave 
· In house leadership development programs with room to grow in a values-driven company
Join our award-winning team, Full-Service Car Care and Repair! Wherever You Drive, We’re Nearby – We have 13 Convenient Triangle Locations across Chapel Hill • Durham • Raleigh • Apex • Cary • Carrboro
We are an equal opportunity employer, and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, disability status, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law

title: Project Manager
description_text:
Job Title: Project Manager (PM) Role SummaryWe are looking for an experienced Project Manager who is highly focused on strict scope management aligned to the Statement of Work (SOW), disciplined delivery of milestones, and strong financial control. The ideal candidate will ensure projects are delivered on time, within budget, with accurate invoicing and financial analysis. Oracle EPM domain knowledge is strongly preferred. Key ResponsibilitiesØ Project Delivery & Scope Management
Own end-to-end project delivery in line with approved SOW, scope, and contractual commitments.
Establish and maintain detailed project plans to meet agreed timelines and milestones.
Proactively manage and control scope creep; ensure all changes follow formal change control processes.
Track deliverables closely and ensure timely, high-quality execution.
Ø Financial Management & Invoicing
Own project budgets, forecasts, and cost tracking.
Monitor burn rate, margins, and overall financial health of projects.
Ensure timely and accurate invoicing aligned with contractual milestones.
Perform financial analysis, variance reporting, and forecasting.
Ø Stakeholder & Governance Management
Act as the primary point of contact for internal and external stakeholders.
Drive project governance, status reporting, and risk/issue management.
Escalate risks and deviations early with clear mitigation plans.
Ø Oracle EPM Coordination (Preferred)
Manage projects involving Oracle EPM solutions (Planning, Budgeting, Forecasting, Financial Close, Reporting).
Coordinate with functional, technical, and finance teams to ensure Oracle EPM deliverables align with project scope and timelines.
Understand Oracle EPM dependencies to support effective planning and risk management.
Required Skills & Experience
12+ years of experience in Project Management.
Strong experience managing projects with tight SOW and scope control.
Proven expertise in budget management, invoicing, and financial analysis.
Experience delivering projects using Waterfall, Agile, or Hybrid methodologies.
Excellent communication, stakeholder management, and organizational skills.
Preferred Qualifications
Prior experience managing Oracle EPM implementations or programs.
PMP / PRINCE2 or equivalent certification.
Experience in consulting or client-facing delivery roles.
Strong understanding of finance and FP&A processes
ai_core_responsibilities: The Project Manager will own end-to-end project delivery, ensuring alignment with the Statement of Work and managing project budgets and financial health. They will also act as the primary point of contact for stakeholders and drive project governance and risk management.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have 12+ years of experience in project management with a strong focus on budget management and financial analysis. Experience with Oracle EPM and relevant certifications like PMP or PRINCE2 are preferred.

title: Reservations Consultant | Cruise Whitsundays
description_text:
About Us
Cruise Whitsundays, based in Airlie Beach is the largest marine-based tour and transport provider in the Whitsundays. Providing close encounters with the Great Barrier Reef, Whitehaven Beach, and the islands of the Whitsundays, Cruise Whitsundays operates 10, well-equipped, air-conditioned vessels, plus a world-class sailing catamaran, offering a range of tours, as well as an inter-island resort connection service, with regular daily departures at two islands, one airport and two mainland ports. 
Cruise Whitsundays also operates Australia’s first underwater accommodation, Reefsuites, and a Reefsleep glamping experience at the Reefworld pontoon, moored offshore at Hardy Reef on the Great Barrier Reef. 
From our cruises and sailing adventures to our world-renowned underwater accommodation, there’s no better way to experience the Whitsundays, and you won’t find a friendlier or more experienced crew to work with. When the ocean is your office, every day is good one!
About Journey Beyond
Australia’s leading experiential tourism group, Journey Beyond, is privileged to operate 17 brands spanning the country, connecting guests to the land, and to each other. Headquartered in Adelaide, Journey Beyond formed in 2016 and now comprises tourism brands including: iconic trains The Ghan, Indian Pacific, Great Southern, The Overland; premium small-group outback operator Outback Spirit; eco-luxury lodge Sal Salis Ningaloo Reef; aquatic adventures Cruise Whitsundays, Rottnest Express, The Paspaley Pearl Farm Tour, Horizontal Falls Seaplane Adventures, Darwin Harbour Cruises and Journey Beyond Cruise Sydney; our tallest members, Melbourne Skydeck and Eureka 89; the historic Vintage Rail Journeys: The Telegraph Station in Alice Springs and Monarto Safari Resort. 
Joining the Journey Beyond family, you will play an integral role in supporting our business as we continue to share special places and shape lasting memories for our guests.
About The Role
We’re on the lookout for a motivated and enthusiastic individual to come aboard as a Reservations Consultant in our vibrant team. This permanent full-time position is based at the stunning Port of Airlie.
As the first point of contact for our valued customers, you’ll play a crucial role in helping them discover and book their dream experiences. Reporting to the Customer Service & Sales Manager, you’ll assist with enquiries and make their journey with us unforgettable.
Key responsibilities will include:
Stay up to date with all things Cruise Whitsundays – products, timetables, and promotions!
Accurately manage bookings from email, phone, web chat, and direct sources using Custom Linc.
Process payment bookings and wrap up end-of-day cash-ups correctly.
Check and finalise QA bookings while applying promotions.
Coordinate bookings with third-party providers with precision.
Keep up to date with our Reservations Policies and Procedures.
Collect and provide catering information
Provide great service in the Retail Store and upsell our fabulous merchandise!
Prepare the necessary paperwork and manifests for the next day’s operations.
Respond to guest feedback in a friendly manner, following our Policies & Procedures.
About You 
You are an enthusiastic and motivated individual with a strong focus on customer service and sales, ready to make a splash in our team! With a dynamic energy that exudes positivity, you genuinely enjoy your work and strive to create memorable experiences for our customers. 
To be successful in this role you will be / have: 
Exceptional Customer Service Skills. You thrive on providing outstanding service, effortlessly converting enquiries into bookings through your wealth of knowledge and a friendly approach.
Confident with technology and communication devices making you efficient in managing various customer interactions, ensuring a seamless experience.
Excellent Communicator in both written and verbal communication skills, you excel in connecting with customers and supplying the information they need to make informed decisions.
Multitask competing priorities, remaining calm under pressure, and always maintaining a solution-based mindset when tackling challenges.
Team player with high energy and bring a positive attitude to your team, fostering a collaborative environment where everyone feels supported and motivated.
Strong attention to detail ensures every task is carried out to the highest standard, contributing to our commitment to excellence.
Minimum 1 year’s Customer Service/Sales experience 
Certificate II in Tourism or Business Admin (desirable)
Familiar with CustomLinc or similar reservation platform (desirable)
Ability to work flexible hours including weekends, evenings and public holidays between the hours of 6:30 am to 7:15 pm
Generous discounts on Journey Beyond travel and experiences 
An attractive salary and positive work environment 
Study Assistance Policy 
A career with opportunities for progression, transfers, and secondments
Employee Referral Policy 
Parental Leave program
Purchased Leave policy
Private Health discounts 
Employee Assistance Program 
Discounts on Journey Beyond merchandise 
Discounts at the Cruise Café & Bar
Discounts at the bar on board vessels
Free public transport
Salary sacrifice remote area benefits
Applications will be reviewed and progressed as they are received, so don't wait, apply now!

Attractive Salary and Positive Work Environment
Career with Opportunities for Progression, Transfers, and Secondments
Discounts at the Cruise Café & Bar
Discounts at the Bar on Board Vessels

ai_requirements_summary: The ideal candidate should have exceptional customer service skills and at least one year of experience in customer service or sales. A Certificate II in Tourism or Business Administration is desirable, along with familiarity with reservation platforms.

Headquartered in Adelaide, Journey Beyond formed in 2016 and now comprises tourism brands including: iconic trains The Ghan, Indian Pacific, Great Southern, The Overland; premium small-group outback operator Outback Spirit; eco-luxury lodge Sal Salis Ningaloo Reef; aquatic adventures Cruise Whitsundays, Rottnest Express, The Paspaley Pearl Farm Tour, Horizontal Falls Seaplane Adventures, Darwin Harbour Cruises, Journey Beyond Cruise Sydney, Exmouth Dive & Whalesharks Ningaloo and Sailaway Port Douglas; our tallest members, Melbourne Skydeck and Eureka 89; the historic Vintage Rail Journeys; The Telegraph Station in Alice Springs; Monarto Safari Resort and New Zealand coach tour specialist Grand Pacific Tours.

title: Shopify Technical Lead
description_text:
Tryzens Limited is a fast-growing IT solutions consultancy head with headquarters based in London and a proven development centre in Trivandrum, India, a near shore office in Sofia, Bulgaria and in Melbourne, Brisbane, Sydney Australia and in Indianapolis, USA. Our areas of expertise include delivery of digital solutions (eCommerce and Non commerce), multi-channel retail solutions and project management amongst others. We provide best of breed solutions and services to several blue-chip clients primarily within retail, financial services, and other industries. We have built our delivery focused reputation upon technical innovation, in-depth business knowledge, and creative vision, all of which supports our objective of helping clients to gain true value from digital solutions. We have a platform-neutral independent approach working with the world’s leading technology partners Shopify, Salesforce, SAP Commerce, Adobe Commerce, BigCommerce, Commercetools and Middleware. Tryzens has been awarded Salesforce Commerce Cloud’s EMEA delivery partner of 2017!
You will be part of our rapidly growing Shopify practice helping to shape and deliver the right outcomes for our clients. You will operate across the project lifecycle from discovery through to development, test, training, and post-implementation support.
The core responsibilities are: 
Development of client solutions
Participate in the discovery phase and provide an estimation of the business requirements.
Planning and coordinating with internal and external stakeholders to ensure project execution as per technical and functional specifications.
Reviewing technical requirements and adapting those into Shopify.
Communicating with client business to fully understand client expectations.
Participating in and leading code-review sessions to identify potential project bottlenecks and suggest solutions.
Engagement in technical design discussions, requirement analysis, coding, testing, implementation and maintenance of Shopify and associated software applications.
Managing technical delivery of integrations and custom extensions.
Providing hands-on support in software/application architecture, development, design, independent analysis, and problem resolution.
Shopify setup, configuration, customization, data Migration, and deployment of applications to Shopify and associated platforms.
Providing guidance to clients and developers over technology, processes, and applications while updating them on regular project-related developments. 
Effectively troubleshoot production issues and do root cause analysis.
Train and integrate new team members and proactively support the continued
development of existing team members.
Build and promote a positive working environment for the team.
Effectively work with team members in line with company policy and procedures,
including performance, development, disciplinary and conflict resolution with
effective feedback
Excellent communication skills

Commercial
Supporting Presales during development of new business with new or existing clients
General
Participate in recruitment activities where necessary.
Maintain certification in Shopify
Essential Skills & Experience
A bachelor’s degree or equivalent. 
Team player with excellent collaboration skills, with a fantastic can-do attitude. 
Self-driven, with an aptitude to learn and keep up with the latest in Shopify.
Must have excellent written and verbal communication skills.
A minimum 4 years’ experience developing on the Shopify including:
Experience in implementing applications in cloud using server less functions, services etc.
Experience in implementing unit tests for NodeJS or PHP
Experience in performance tuning of Nodejs or PHP applications and writing high performance code
Experience in addressing quality/security issues in Nodejs/PHP code
Good exposure towards test-driven development while using the industry best practices with an ability to write new test suites
Produce code that is easy to read and maintain.
Proven track record in software development environments and experiences of end-to-end project lifecycles.
Proficient in co-coordinating with teams and clients across different time zones.
Familiar with JIRA, Confluence, MS Outlook, Word, Excel, Visio, and Project
Overall, 7 to 12 Years of IT experience.
Knowledge in other ecommerce platform like Magento, BigCommerce, Commercetools will be preferred.
Knowledge in technologies such as Vue.js, React.js, Ruby language (Ruby on Rails framework) for creating apps or scheduler jobs.
Good understanding of functionalities and features provided by Shopify.
Experience with custom apps and integrations available in Shopify.
Strong knowledge of Liquid template language 
Strong hands-on experience in customizing Shopify themes.
Knowledge in technologies such as NodeJS OR PHP framework like Laravel, ExpressJs HTML5, CSS3, JavaScript/Jquery.
Experience with implementing/debugging Shopify apps as well as creating custom extensions.
Experience with Shopify's object/properties, REST API, webhooks and Meta fields
Clear Understanding on Shopify concepts

linkedin_org_description:
Tryzens Global is a new kind of digital commerce agency. Big enough to matter, but small enough to care.

We’re leaders in global branded commerce, helping international brands sell their goods and services to millions of customers, in their new and existing markets.

locations_alt_raw: New York, New York, United States
locations_derived: New York, New York, United States
description_text:
Head of Commercial Operations
Manhattan, New York
On Target First-Year Earnings: $200,000 ($130,000 base + $70,000 uncapped performance-based bonus)
Meaningful early equity at our ground-floor valuation with >100x upside potential
In office with flexible remote days
About Aurora
Aurora is the travel & lifestyle management operating system for the world’s highest-performance founders, athletes, and family offices. Backed by the most successful founder in the space ever.
Our mobile app empowers ultra-high-performance individuals and their teams to master their relationships, exploration, and longevity across five lifestyle categories: travel, dining, experiences, luxury goods, and longevity.
To date, we have:
Raised $5M from Tier-1 investors and operators, including the founder of Cleo, Quadpay, early investors in Supercell and Deliveroo, the President of VistaJet, and tier-1 athletes including Kevin Love and Kyle Kuzma
Achieved industry-leading engagement among paying members, with a large waitlist seeking access
Built a proactive, seamless, conversational product frequently described as “magic” by our members
We’re now building our Commercial Operations function as we scale our product and membership base.
The Aurora Head of Commercial Operations
Want to build the world’s most revered premium supplier ecosystem across dining, travel, wellness, luxury goods, and experiences?
Aurora is seeking a Head of Commercial Operations to own our supplier engine: attracting and nurturing the relationships that unlock unmatched intelligence, off-market access, and extraordinary member experiences.
You’ll build the brand, incentives, and contribute to the systems to scale this system globally.
This is a ground-floor role for a creative operator ready to shape the future of premium lifestyle.
What you’ll do
Build Aurora’s supplier network into a living ecosystem that attracts the world’s best hospitality and lifestyle partners.
Craft the brand, community, and relationships that make Aurora culturally magnetic within the industry.
Design incentive structures and rewards that drive exceptional partner performance and outcomes. Build performance systems that provide clear visibility into top suppliers and where Aurora should deepen investment over time.
Partner closely with Aurora’s founding team, product function, and operations team to build the infrastructure that makes it seamless for partners to engage with Aurora and deliver differentiated intelligence, access, and perks.
This role reports directly to Aurora’s Co-Founder & COO and has clear potential to grow rapidly into a leadership position spanning Commercial Operations, Member Experience and Product.
Responsibilities:
Own Aurora’s supplier platform end-to-end, spanning sourcing, nurturing, and scaling premium relationships over time
Design incentive structures, gifting programs, and rewards that motivate exceptional partner performance
Craft Aurora’s supplier-facing brand and ecosystem through community, events, and cultural touchpoints that deepen loyalty
Build special relationships with the world’s best hospitality and lifestyle insiders to unlock differentiated access, intelligence, and experiences for Aurora members
Negotiate and formalize high-impact partnerships that expand Aurora’s global supplier network across our core verticals
Implement performance tracking, relationship health metrics, and operational rigor to identify where to lean in or cut off
Partner closely with Aurora’s founding team, product function, and operations team to build the systems and infrastructure that scale this network globally
Who you are:
Creative, with the taste and storytelling ability to build a compelling partner-oriented brand
A relentless relationship-builder and closer who knows how to get in the door and turn “no” into a “yes”
A natural community builder who can create a magnetic ecosystem that partners want to belong to
Deeply networked in lifestyle and hospitality worlds, with strong empathy for how top operators and insiders think
Operationally minded, with the ability to transform bespoke partnerships into scalable programs and systems
An owner at heart, eager to take full responsibility for building and running Aurora’s Commercial Operations function
Competitive salary with uncapped performance-based bonus
Founding stock options package
Flexible remote days
Lifestyle access perks and access to exclusive events & Aurora services
Whoop or Oura Ring on us
$2,000 annual education budget
Longevity subscription
Whatever tech you need to do your best work
Generous parental leave
Comprehensive health benefits, with 90% coverage, including dental and vision

linkedin_org_slogan: The AI-Native lifestyle operating system for the world's highest-performers.
linkedin_org_description:
We’re pushing premium lifestyle commerce toward true autonomy - backed by the most successful founder this space has ever seen.

Dining, travel, experiences, gifting, and wellness sit at the center of our professional, personal, and social lives. Demand for authentic, standout experiences has never been higher.

Yet more than $1.5 trillion in premium lifestyle spend remains fragmented, inefficient, and ultimately unfulfilling.

The next step isn’t another booking app, credit card, or pay-to-play marketplace.

It’s intelligent agents that proactively curate high-leverage, hyper-personalized opportunities globally - managing access, logistics, payments, perks, and personalization on our behalf, without needing to be prompted.

For the first time in history, we have the tools to ingest and synthesize millions of real-world data points into a single operating system - one that understands objectives, health state, preferences, and constraints, and executes with precision and speed across lifestyle domains, geographies, and networks.

Doing the work for us so we can focus directly on the people, places, and experiences that compound our interdisciplinary mastery.

Think Anduril’s Lattice, applied not to warfare - but to life.

Multi-modal, multi-party, real-time lifestyle orchestration is the future for the world’s leading founders, athletes, family offices, and brands.

So we’re building it.

This is our life’s work. And we’re looking for ambitious, mission-driven travel managers, lifestyle managers, and engineers to join us.

We’re backed by tier-1 VCs and operators.

We’re an in-person company, building from our headquarters in Midtown Manhattan, with offices in Belgravia, London, and West Hollywood, Los Angeles.

If you want to help define an entirely new category - and build something enduring - this is the moment.

Join us.

title: IT Senior Project Manager
description_text:
We are seeking an experienced Senior Project Manager to lead complex, high-impact projects from initiation through delivery. In this role, you will be responsible for driving project success through strategic planning, stakeholder management, and team leadership while ensuring projects are completed on time, within scope, and on budget.
Role & Responsibilities
The Senior Project Manager will lead end-to-end project delivery, developing comprehensive project plans that include timelines, resource allocation, budgets, and risk mitigation strategies. You will serve as the primary point of contact for stakeholders at all levels, facilitating clear communication and managing expectations throughout the project lifecycle.
You will build and lead cross-functional project teams, fostering collaboration and accountability while mentoring junior project managers and team members. Critical responsibilities include identifying and proactively managing project risks and issues, developing mitigation strategies, and making data-driven decisions to keep projects on track.
You will establish and track key performance indicators, providing regular status updates and reports to executive leadership and stakeholders. Additionally, you will manage project budgets and resource allocation to optimize efficiency and return on investment, while ensuring adherence to organizational standards, methodologies, and best practices.
Required Qualifications
Candidates should possess a Bachelor's degree in Business, Engineering, Computer Science, or a related field, along with 7+ years of project management experience with a proven track record of successfully delivering complex projects. PMP, PRINCE2, or equivalent certification is plus, and Agile/Scrum certification is strongly preferred.
You must demonstrate expert knowledge of project management methodologies, tools, and best practices, with proficiency in project management software such as Jira, Asana, or similar platforms. Strong leadership abilities with experience managing cross-functional teams of 10+ members are essential, as are exceptional communication and interpersonal skills with the ability to influence at all organizational levels.
The ideal candidate will have proven experience managing project budgets of $1M+ and possess strong analytical and problem-solving capabilities with attention to detail.
Preferred Qualifications
We value experience in our specific industry or domain, advanced degree (MBA or Master's), and experience with change management and organizational transformation initiatives. Background in both Waterfall and Agile methodologies, along with experience managing vendor relationships and external partners, is advantageous.
ABOUT US
GoGlobal is the leading professional services company to guide, establish and manage global operations with compliance and confidence. We bring the human touch to entering new markets and consolidating vendors through a single point of management. With a presence in over 140 countries on six continents and growing, we make running a global business easy.
Business & Corporate Services provided:
Entity Solutions: Entity establishment, Corporate secretarial & directorship services, Domicile services, Dormancy/Liquidation
HR Solutions: Global recruitment, Employer of Record (EOR), Agent of Record (AOR), Global benefits, Global payroll, International HR Consulting
Finance Solutions: Accounting & Tax
For more information, please visit GoGlobal.com
OVERVIEW
We Measure Success by Results, Not Hours Worked
No timesheets.
At GoGlobal, we give you the autonomy to make decisions that create real impact. We prioritize results over hours worked, focusing on quality of outcomes rather than tracking time.
As a privately owned company, we are free from the pressures of venture capital or private equity investors demanding exponential growth in unreasonable time frames.
Sometimes, things just take time to do well. In our business, delivering great results is a process - and we're committed to doing it right.
We are not for sale. We believe that financial success is a natural outcome of our work, not the primary goal. Unlike many investors who view money as a goal, our focus is on long-term growth and sustainable success.
We are a global team, built for those who want to go further. Here's what your career at GoGlobal looks like:
A company small enough for your voice to be heard meaningfully, yet large enough to make a global impact
Collaborating with a diverse, global and supportive team
Built for the long-haul - no VC or PE pressure, just a focus on sustainable growth
WHY WE ARE DIFFERENT
We Make Growing Your Career Possible
We operate with the benefits of a partnership without the complexities of a partnership. Our hybrid partnership-corporate model offers all employees the opportunity to become a partner, while maintaining a streamlined corporate structure so that decisions are made quickly
We do not offer equity plans. As we are not for sale and are not chasing an exit, "stock options" hold little value in our business model. Instead, we provide opportunities for outstanding employees to make partner and share in the profits. We believe it's better to build lasting value than to chase a one-time payout
We prioritize organic and sustainable growth. Expanding operations quickly by way of mergers and acquisitions might seem appealing, but the reality is integrating diverse cultures can be challenging. We're proud of our own culture and prefer to grow it our way
We're global. We offer career opportunities across the world-whether it's new job roles or international locations
English is our official language, but for many, it's our second language. We embrace this and find it rewarding communicating with colleagues from all over the world
We are not a family. We operate more like a high-performing sports team where each individual's contribution is crucial to our collective success
We keep things simple and collaborative. Our structure makes it easy for teams to work together across borders so we can serve our clients with global needs more effectively. Unlike big, siloed companies, we work seamlessly to handle international deals without the headaches of territorial ownership
We believe in transparency and trust. Every month, we share our financials with the team so everyone knows where we stand and what's coming next. It's all about open communication, building trust and empowering every individual to be part of our journey. We don't tell you what to think - we provide the information you need to make your own informed decisions
We believe flexibility drives success. Our company is built on trust, and that's why we embrace fully remote work, and hybrid options in some countries. Forget rigid office routines - it's old school! This flexibility fuels productivity, strengthens our workforce, and ensures we remain agile in a fast-changing world.
Ready to take the next step in your career with us? Apply now!
**Kindly review the provided privacy notice (LINK) to gain a comprehensive understanding of our policies and practices governing the treatment of your Personal Data.

As a Junior Specialist in Pediatrics, you will work in a collaborative environment, assisting experienced pediatricians in diagnosing and managing a variety of health conditions. You will have the chance to engage with young patients and their families, providing vital support and education about health and wellness.
Key Responsibilities:
Assist in the evaluation and management of pediatric patients under the supervision of senior specialists.
Conduct routine health assessments and provide preventive care, including vaccinations and developmental screenings.
Participate in the development and implementation of individualized treatment plans.
Educate families on health, nutrition, and developmental milestones.
Document patient interactions and maintain accurate medical records in alignment with facility protocols.
Collaborate with multidisciplinary teams to ensure comprehensive patient care.
Engage in continuous professional development and attend training sessions to enhance skills.
Medical degree (MD/DO) from an accredited institution and completion of a pediatric residency program.
Eligibility for board certification in Pediatrics or actively pursuing certification.
Possession of a valid medical license in the relevant jurisdiction.
Strong interest in pediatric healthcare, particularly in delivering care to children.
Excellent communication skills, especially for engaging with children and their parents.
Ability to work collaboratively within a healthcare team while demonstrating a willingness to learn.
Detail-oriented with strong organizational and time management skills.
Commitment to providing patient-centered, compassionate care.
Openness to continuous education and staying informed about developments in pediatrics.
Private Health Insurance
Training & Development
Performance Bonus

I started Water Restoration Marketing with one clear goal: to make it ridiculously simple for water restoration business owners to get consistent, high-quality calls from people who actually need their services. For over six years, we’ve worked exclusively with restoration companies, combining paid ads and Google Maps optimization to generate more than 250,000 real-time calls for over 200 businesses across the U.S.
Today, we’re a globally distributed team across the U.S., Philippines, South Africa, India, and Pakistan. What makes us different is how we think about scale - we’re building an AI-forward agency that grows through automation and smarter systems, not endless headcount. We value ownership, initiative, and getting things done, and we genuinely enjoy working with people who care deeply about doing great work.
We’re looking for an Automation / Mar-tech Specialist. 
You’re someone who can’t stand inefficient processes and gets genuinely excited about replacing manual work with clean, reliable automations. This role exists to remove bottlenecks, speed up execution, and help our team focus on higher-value strategic and creative work as we scale through automation.
In this role, you will:
⚙️ Build and maintain automations using Zapier, n8n, and APIs
🔁 Execute and improve workflows designed by leadership
🧩 Create custom integrations and scripts where native tools fall short
🛠 Troubleshoot, optimize, and improve existing automation workflows
📝 Document automation processes and ensure long-term system reliability
This is a full-time, non-customer-facing role with flexible hours, requiring 3–4 hours of overlap with US Eastern Time (ideally between 6am–2pm ET).
This role will report to Jeff Duldulao, Co-Founder & COO. 
Jeff is a hands-on operator and automation-first leader with 20+ years of experience in technology, systems architecture, and process optimization. He has built and scaled multiple businesses across digital marketing, home services, retail, and franchising, with a strong focus on using automation to eliminate manual work and improve operational efficiency. 
Fun fact: Jeff is known for getting overly excited when a new automation successfully replaces a manual process - especially if it saves hours of work!
Must-haves:
✅ Proven experience with automation tools like Zapier and/or n8n
✅ Experience working with APIs and building integrations
✅ Experience in a marketing agency or MarTech environment (e.g. conversion tracking, UTM tags)
✅ Hands-on experience using AI tools such as ChatGPT or Claude
Brownie points:
⭐ Scripting experience (Python, JavaScript, or similar)
⭐ Strong API architecture knowledge for custom integrations
⭐ Portfolio or GitHub showcasing automation or scripting projects
You’re a self-starter with a sharp eye for detail who can also see the bigger picture. You take ownership, move fast without waiting for perfect instructions, and naturally look for better, smarter ways to do things. You care deeply about quality, follow through on what you start, and enjoy learning, experimenting, and improving over time.
What’s in it for you?
💰 $3,000 - $3,500 per month*
🌍 Full-time, fully remote role
🏖 Minimum 20 days paid time off (including public holidays of your choice)
⏰ Flexible, trust-based work schedule
🤖 Work in an AI-forward, innovation-focused environment
🚀 Opportunity to grow into workflow design and higher-impact automation work

title: Legal Counsel
description_text:
The Legal Counsel will be responsible for overseeing the legal operations and compliance of the entire group of companies, including the parent company and its subsidiaries. This role requires a experience and strategic legal professional who can advise senior management, manage legal risks, ensure regulatory compliance, and oversee complex legal matters across multiple jurisdictions.
Management and development of the Legal Function: Managing and developing the legal and compliance function across the group in addition to managing and supervising team members
Legal Advisor to the Executive Team: Advising senior management and members of the Executive Board on legal and strategic matters across the Group
Legal Compliance: Ensure that business activities are conducted in conformity with all applicable laws, regulations, internal policies and procedures.
Contract Lifecycle: Oversee the entire contract lifecycle and contract management process and tool. Actively participate in reviewing, drafting and negotiating contracts in addition to maintaining and developing the Group’s legal templates and observance of standard terms of contracting. Support ongoing contract management processes to mitigate risk and impact of contract breaches and customer complaints.
Litigation and Dispute Resolution: manage disputes, litigation, regulatory investigations and arbitration proceedings and manage external counsel
Corporate Governance: ensure compliance with corporate governance standards and support corporate governance processes and record keeping.
Regulatory Affairs Oversee and ensure the Group’s compliance with regulatory requirements and industry specific rules and regulations and advise management on regulatory requirements and developments. Liaise with regulatory bodies and maintain relationships with relevant industry bodies and trade associations where applicable. Ensure regulatory compliance requirements are reflected in company policies, processes, SLAs.
Compliance Oversee the Group’s compliance function including drafting, implementing and managing core Group compliance policies (e.g. AML and CFT Policies and privacy compliance (including GDPR and similar privacy regulations), implementing and overseeing procedures.
Risk Management Continual compliance risk assessment of company practices together with the development of internal policies and procedures, compliance training and protocols, managing legal and compliance risks and reporting to the Executive Board/risk committee.
Business Process Enhancement: Actively participate in business process engineering exercises 
Manage External Consultants: Liaise with external lawyers, auditors, tax advisors, bankers and other external consultants
Training and Awareness: Provide suitable training and awareness to staff in relation to legal and compliance policies and processes
LLB Degree
Proven track record (5+ years) as Group Legal Counsel or similar senior inhouse legal role
Experience in a multinational or large regional private practice firm 
Experience in TMT (Technology Media & Telecommunications) sector and/or digital payments and digital content distribution 
Experience dealing with Telecom Operators and Regulations 
Experience working across multiple jurisdictions (in particular Europe and MEA)
ai_core_responsibilities: The Legal Counsel will oversee legal operations and compliance for the group of companies, advising senior management and managing legal risks. Responsibilities include contract management, litigation, corporate governance, and regulatory compliance.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must have a minimum of 5 years of experience as Group Legal Counsel or in a similar senior in-house legal role, preferably in the TMT sector. An LLB degree is required, along with experience across multiple jurisdictions.

title: Account Manager (Fulfillment Owner)
description_text:
This is not a traditional Account Manager role.
At our client, Account Managers own fulfillment end-to-end and are accountable for client success, performance, and growth.
They do not personally run ads or build GHL funnels but everything that happens in fulfillment runs through them.
ROLE SUMMARY
An Account Manager is the owner of the client outcome.
From the moment a client pays until they are consistently growing revenue, the Account Manager is responsible for:
Making sure every step of fulfillment happens correctly
Coordinating specialists (media buyers, copywriters, editors, GHL experts) 
Proactively communicating progress and performance
Catching issues early and fixing them
Ensuring clients see results, clarity, and momentum
If fulfillment breaks, stalls, or underperforms this role owns fixing it.
WHAT THEY ARE / ARE NOT
THEY ARE:
The single point of ownership for the client
Responsible for success, performance, and retention
A coordinator of specialists, not a doer of specialist tasks
Measured on results, not friendliness
THEY ARE NOT:
The media buyer
The GHL expert
The copywriter
The editor
Those roles report into fulfillment, but the Account Manager owns the outcome.
CORE RESPONSIBILITIES (END-TO-END FULFILLMENT) Account Managers are responsible for every step of fulfillment, including:
Owning the client relationship after close
Running onboarding and ensuring systems are live
Coordinating content production, ads, and funnels
Enforcing timelines and deliverables
Reviewing work for quality and brand alignment
Green-lighting launches
Monitoring performance and pipeline health
Communicating results and next steps weekly
They ensure nothing falls through the cracks.
PERFORMANCE OWNERSHIP (NON-NEGOTIABLE) Account Managers are accountable for:
Client growth and retention
Appointment volume and pipeline health
Cost per appointment efficiency
Content velocity and quality
Clear weekly communication
They are expected to understand performance metrics, even if they are not the technical executor.
THE 10 CORE HABITS WE EXPECT (BUILT INTO THE ROLE)
Great HIVE Account Managers consistently demonstrate these behaviors:
1.Frequent, proactive communication
Clients never chase for updates, weekly updates are standard. 
2. Strong relationship ownership
They build trust while still holding boundaries.
3.Brand alignment vigilance
They ensure all content and ads “feel like the client.”
4.Obsessed with results
Performance is always visible and explained.
5.Proactive problem-solving
Issues are raised early with solutions, not excuses.
6.Clear expectations & transparency
No over-promising. No surprises.
7.Extreme ownership
No blame-passing. Ever.
8.Acts as an extension of the client’s team
Thinks in “we,” not “you.”
9.Consistency over time
Same level of care in month six as month one.
10. Listens to and implements feedback
Clients feel heard and see action taken.
REQUIRED BACKGROUND
Candidates must have experience in at least one of the following:
Marketing or lead generation agency
High-ticket service fulfillment
Managing multiple client accounts simultaneously
Performance-driven or operations-driven environments They must be comfortable managing 10-15 active clients.
REQUIRED TRAITS
We are looking for people who are:
Highly organized
Comfortable enforcing deadlines
Calm under pressure
Process-driven
Metrics-aware
Ownership-oriented
Success in this role is measured by:
Client retention
Performance consistency
Fulfillment execution quality
Clear, proactive communication
Minimal escalation needed
This role is best suited for someone who:
Wants real responsibility
Is comfortable being accountable
Enjoys running systems and people
Takes pride in client wins
This is not a passive role.
This is an ownership role.
Fully Remote 
Time Zone: US working hours
Salary $2000.00- $3000.00
ai_core_responsibilities: Account Managers are responsible for every step of fulfillment, ensuring that all processes are executed correctly and that clients achieve their desired outcomes. They coordinate specialists, monitor performance, and maintain clear communication with clients.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must have experience in marketing or lead generation, managing multiple client accounts, and working in performance-driven environments. They should be highly organized, calm under pressure, and comfortable enforcing deadlines.

title: Deckhand | Sailaway Port Douglas
description_text:
Be a part of sharing special places and shaping lasting memories with Australia’s leading experiential tourism business.
About Us
At Sailaway, we’re passionate about sharing the beauty and wonder of the Great Barrier Reef. Our mission is to provide guests with an unforgettable experience through expert reef interpretation and sustainable sailing adventures.
Our team brings together passionate experts who deliver engaging and informative presentations, helping guests appreciate the fragile marine environment and the incredible life beneath the surface. Complementing this knowledge are skilled professionals who ensure safety and comfort while delivering the thrill of pure sailing aboard state-of-the-art catamarans.
Based in Port Douglas, we offer daily access to spectacular reef sites, including Mackay Coral Cay and the idyllic Low Isles. These locations feature vibrant coral gardens, giant clams, and abundant marine life, making them perfect for snorkellers.
We pride ourselves on exceptional customer service, sustainability, and creating memorable experiences for every guest.
About Journey Beyond
Australia’s leading experiential tourism group, Journey Beyond, is privileged to operate 20 brands spanning the country and 
New Zealand, connecting guests to the land, and to each other. 
Headquartered in Adelaide, Journey Beyond formed in 2016 and now comprises tourism brands including: iconic trains The Ghan, Indian Pacific, Great Southern, The Overland; premium small-group outback operator Outback Spirit; eco-luxury lodge Sal Salis Ningaloo Reef; aquatic adventures Cruise Whitsundays, Rottnest Express, Horizontal Falls Seaplane Adventures, Darwin Harbour Cruises and Journey Beyond Cruise Sydney; our tallest members, Melbourne Skydeck and Eureka 89; the historic Vintage Rail Journeys; The Telegraph Station in Alice Springs; Monarto Safari Resort; Exmouth Dive & Whalesharks Ningaloo and New Zealand coach tour specialist Grand Pacific Tours.
About The Role
We are seeking a First Deckhand to join our team on a permanent part time basis. As a crucial member of our team, the First Deckhand will provide exemplary customer service while ensuring the safety and comfort of all passengers on board. You will be the first point of contact for our customers, engaging them with information about our Sailaway products and services. In this hands-on role, you will manage various operational aspects while maintaining a high standard of cleanliness and safety throughout the vessel.
Key Responsibilities will include:
Deliver exceptional customer service, maintaining professionalism and a friendly disposition while engaging with passengers.
Manage the passenger boarding process, ensuring accuracy in manifest checks, money collection, and headcounts.
Conduct safety briefs, snorkelling briefs, and guided tours with a focus on environmental interpretation and encourage passenger participation in activities.
Conduct memorable Glass Bottom Boat Tours
Ensure the vessel and all equipment are cleaned, stored, and ready for use, adhering to food safety regulations for catering.
Assist in emergency response drills and ensure safety management systems are strictly followed, while being prepared to provide first aid and support if needed.
Complete incident reports accurately and in a timely manner and maintain the First Aid Kit and oxygen supplies.
About You 
The ideal candidate will be an enthusiastic individual who possesses a solid level of sailing experience and boating knowledge, alongside a comprehensive understanding of safety management systems and vessel handling. You have a strong grasp of snorkelling procedures, reef interpretation, and the history of Low Isles, combined with the ability to confidently lead guided snorkel tours and island heritage walks. Your excellent communication skills allow you to engage with a diverse range of passengers, including children, showcasing patience and professionalism in all interactions.
To be successful you will have:
Previous experience working on a vessel or within the tourism industry
Exceptional organisational and time management abilities
Well-presented and hardworking, with a positive attitude and a willingness to learn new skills
Flexibility to adapt to varying work and customer requirements, demonstrating initiative and responsibility
The ability to thrive both independently and as part of a team and be physically fit and capable of working in hot environments.
Coxswain Grade 1 NC
Current Senior First Aid/CPR/O2 Certificate
Responsible Service of Alcohol Certificate
Food Hygiene Certificate 
Advanced Reef Teach Course Certificate is desired (sufficient coral and marine life knowledge)
Generous discounts on Journey Beyond travel and experiences 
Study Assistance Policy 
Employee Referral Policy 
Parental Leave program
Purchased Leave policy
Private Health discounts 
Employee Assistance Program 
Discounts on Journey Beyond merchandise 
Applications will be processed in the order they are received, so don’t delay, apply now!

title: IT Senior Project Manager
description_text:
We are seeking an experienced Senior Project Manager to lead complex, high-impact projects from initiation through delivery. In this role, you will be responsible for driving project success through strategic planning, stakeholder management, and team leadership while ensuring projects are completed on time, within scope, and on budget.
Role & Responsibilities
The Senior Project Manager will lead end-to-end project delivery, developing comprehensive project plans that include timelines, resource allocation, budgets, and risk mitigation strategies. You will serve as the primary point of contact for stakeholders at all levels, facilitating clear communication and managing expectations throughout the project lifecycle.
You will build and lead cross-functional project teams, fostering collaboration and accountability while mentoring junior project managers and team members. Critical responsibilities include identifying and proactively managing project risks and issues, developing mitigation strategies, and making data-driven decisions to keep projects on track.
You will establish and track key performance indicators, providing regular status updates and reports to executive leadership and stakeholders. Additionally, you will manage project budgets and resource allocation to optimize efficiency and return on investment, while ensuring adherence to organizational standards, methodologies, and best practices.
Required Qualifications
Candidates should possess a Bachelor's degree in Business, Engineering, Computer Science, or a related field, along with 7+ years of project management experience with a proven track record of successfully delivering complex projects. PMP, PRINCE2, or equivalent certification is plus, and Agile/Scrum certification is strongly preferred.
You must demonstrate expert knowledge of project management methodologies, tools, and best practices, with proficiency in project management software such as Jira, Asana, or similar platforms. Strong leadership abilities with experience managing cross-functional teams of 10+ members are essential, as are exceptional communication and interpersonal skills with the ability to influence at all organizational levels.
The ideal candidate will have proven experience managing project budgets of $1M+ and possess strong analytical and problem-solving capabilities with attention to detail.
Preferred Qualifications
We value experience in our specific industry or domain, advanced degree (MBA or Master's), and experience with change management and organizational transformation initiatives. Background in both Waterfall and Agile methodologies, along with experience managing vendor relationships and external partners, is advantageous.
ABOUT US
GoGlobal is the leading professional services company to guide, establish and manage global operations with compliance and confidence. We bring the human touch to entering new markets and consolidating vendors through a single point of management. With a presence in over 140 countries on six continents and growing, we make running a global business easy.
Business & Corporate Services provided:
Entity Solutions: Entity establishment, Corporate secretarial & directorship services, Domicile services, Dormancy/Liquidation
HR Solutions: Global recruitment, Employer of Record (EOR), Agent of Record (AOR), Global benefits, Global payroll, International HR Consulting
Finance Solutions: Accounting & Tax
For more information, please visit GoGlobal.com
OVERVIEW
We Measure Success by Results, Not Hours Worked
No timesheets.
At GoGlobal, we give you the autonomy to make decisions that create real impact. We prioritize results over hours worked, focusing on quality of outcomes rather than tracking time.
As a privately owned company, we are free from the pressures of venture capital or private equity investors demanding exponential growth in unreasonable time frames.
Sometimes, things just take time to do well. In our business, delivering great results is a process - and we're committed to doing it right.
We are not for sale. We believe that financial success is a natural outcome of our work, not the primary goal. Unlike many investors who view money as a goal, our focus is on long-term growth and sustainable success.
We are a global team, built for those who want to go further. Here's what your career at GoGlobal looks like:
A company small enough for your voice to be heard meaningfully, yet large enough to make a global impact
Collaborating with a diverse, global and supportive team
Built for the long-haul - no VC or PE pressure, just a focus on sustainable growth
WHY WE ARE DIFFERENT
We Make Growing Your Career Possible
We operate with the benefits of a partnership without the complexities of a partnership. Our hybrid partnership-corporate model offers all employees the opportunity to become a partner, while maintaining a streamlined corporate structure so that decisions are made quickly
We do not offer equity plans. As we are not for sale and are not chasing an exit, "stock options" hold little value in our business model. Instead, we provide opportunities for outstanding employees to make partner and share in the profits. We believe it's better to build lasting value than to chase a one-time payout
We prioritize organic and sustainable growth. Expanding operations quickly by way of mergers and acquisitions might seem appealing, but the reality is integrating diverse cultures can be challenging. We're proud of our own culture and prefer to grow it our way
We're global. We offer career opportunities across the world-whether it's new job roles or international locations
English is our official language, but for many, it's our second language. We embrace this and find it rewarding communicating with colleagues from all over the world
We are not a family. We operate more like a high-performing sports team where each individual's contribution is crucial to our collective success
We keep things simple and collaborative. Our structure makes it easy for teams to work together across borders so we can serve our clients with global needs more effectively. Unlike big, siloed companies, we work seamlessly to handle international deals without the headaches of territorial ownership
We believe in transparency and trust. Every month, we share our financials with the team so everyone knows where we stand and what's coming next. It's all about open communication, building trust and empowering every individual to be part of our journey. We don't tell you what to think - we provide the information you need to make your own informed decisions
We believe flexibility drives success. Our company is built on trust, and that's why we embrace fully remote work, and hybrid options in some countries. Forget rigid office routines - it's old school! This flexibility fuels productivity, strengthens our workforce, and ensures we remain agile in a fast-changing world.
Ready to take the next step in your career with us? Apply now!
**Kindly review the provided privacy notice (LINK) to gain a comprehensive understanding of our policies and practices governing the treatment of your Personal Data.

title: Head of Offline (Agency & Brokerage)
locations_derived: Special capital Region of Jakarta, Java, Indonesia
description_text:
About Igloo
Igloo (formerly Axinan) is the first full-stack insurtech firm to emerge from Singapore. With offices across Singapore, Indonesia, Thailand, the Philippines, Vietnam, Malaysia, China and India, Igloo is fueled by a mission of making insurance accessible and affordable for all. Igloo's innovation solutions empower companies to eliminate operational risk, drive new revenue streams and optimise and enhance their existing products and services. With a successful Pre-Series C fund round of US$36 million in Dec 2023, Igloo has now raised over US$100 million. The leading regional insurtech firm looks to expand regionally with strategic acquisitions and hiring great talent.
As a passionate team building meaningful solutions to make an impact, we are looking for like-minded individuals to join us at Igloo. At our core, we prioritise teamwork and foster a culture built on strong collaboration and we are looking for talented people like you.
Job Responsibilities 
1. Strategic Leadership & Business Growth:
Develop and implement brokerage business strategies to achieve the business KPIs for Direct-to-Business and Partner/Agent Sales teams.
Ensure business plans for each business channel are aligned with commercial goals, focusing on growth and profitability.
Monitor the performance of all teams to ensure the brokerage business hits its sales and revenue targets.
2. Sales & Client Acquisition:
Supervise and closely track the performance of the Direct-to-Business and Agency channel
Sales teams, ensuring alignment with sales targets on a weekly, monthly and quarterly basis.
Oversee and optimize sales processes to ensure pipelines and opportunities growth to drive profitable business
Ensure all teams are driving client acquisition effectively and work towards exceeding revenue goals.
3. Insurance Placement & Partnerships:
Collaborate with the Head of Placement to secure optimal terms from insurers and maintain strong partnerships.
Ensure the placement team provides the best commercial offers on time, supporting the brokerage in meeting customer needs.
Support placement team with new binder development to support sales channel needs.
Use your network in the insurance industry to enhance support to the brokerage business
4. Operational Excellence & Financial Oversight:
Oversee finance reconciliation, ensuring timely and accurate reporting to the Regional Office.
Monitor and enhance reporting systems, ensuring data accuracy and transparency, particularly for monthly numbers and KPIs.
Ensure compliance with all standard operating procedures (SOPs) and continuously improve processes to support business growth.
5. Team Leadership & Performance Management:
Lead the brokerage team, working with the Head of Sales, Head of Placement, and other leaders to ensure targets are met.
Conduct regular performance reviews, ensuring the teams stay on track to meet goals, and provide mentorship to improve team output.
Conduct regular checkups with the Regional Office to assess business health, review performance metrics, and ensure that the brokerage business is on track.
Other roles and responsibilities as determined by Regional Office from time to time.
Minimum Bachelor's degree in business, marketing, or a related field
Minimum of 10 years of experience in the insurance industry with proven track record in building, scaling, and managing agency and/or brokerage channels
Hold professional relevant Indonesian insurance certifications (APARI) is a significant advantage
Deep understanding of OJK regulations and compliance standards
Strong experience in non-life Agency and Broking for both B2B and/or B2C insurance distribution models
Experience working in an Insurtech or a digitally-forward brokerage environment 
Strong knowledge of insurance products, distribution channels, and market trends.
Strong leadership, interpersonal, and communication skills, with the ability to build and maintain relationships with partners and stakeholders
Proven ability to lead multi-functional teams.

description_text:
Disclaimer: This is not a job advertisement. It is an invitation for individuals who aspire to create meaningful change through entrepreneurship as a path to building next generation industries.
Reapra is a pre-ideation venture builder, currently looking for individuals who want to build industries that support the next generation. Unlike traditional investors, Reapra places high focus on nurturing founders through deep understanding of their life stories, purpose, life mission, learning tendencies and long term motivation towards industry building. By nurturing founders through extensive focus on themselves, Reapra wants to support the learning transformation of individuals to manage the complexities of industry building and to work towards industry building for the betterment of society. 
Apply to us if you, 
Want to start (or restart) your entrepreneurial journey in the next 6-12 months 
Desire to be mission and purpose driven in both your short term and long term endeavours. 
Want to create a better future for the next generation through entrepreneurship and industry building. 
Are experiencing nudges that a focus on transforming yourself and your learning tendencies is necessary in entrepreneurship. You are keen to learn new learning concepts and are willing to challenge your existing skills, strengths, experiences or proven best practices
Are deeply introspective. You have deep awareness and articulation of your inner thought processes and emotions related to your actions / reactions. You also want to spend time deeply understanding yourself and how you think, operate and learn. 
Hold legal authorisations to set up a business in Malaysia. Individuals who are citizens or legal permanent residents of Malaysia and are legally allowed to set up a business in Malaysia 
Sounds like you? Or know someone who might be interested? Apply now for a chance to meet with us!

title: Account Manager (Digital)
description_text:
Our client is a unique marketing agency providing end-to-end lead gen services for a focused client base.
Each of their 50 employees understand we are a performance-based agency, which means our monthly client revenue increases when we win. Winning big usually hinges on our ability to provide high-performing direct-response ads, landing pages that convert, and nurture sequences that move leads toward conversion.
As an Account Manager, you will act as the strategic partner and primary point of contact for key clients, cultivating relationships, driving client satisfaction, and ensuring the successful delivery of marketing initiatives that achieve measurable business outcomes.
You will manage accounts with an ownership mindset, overseeing all aspects of client engagement from onboarding to execution and reporting. You’ll collaborate with internal teams and stakeholders across departments to ensure alignment on goals, deliverables, and KPIs. Your ability to combine strategic thinking with exceptional communication and leadership will be critical to maintaining client trust and achieving sustainable growth.
An ideal candidate possesses executive presence, strong analytical acumen, and the ability to translate complex client objectives into actionable marketing strategies. You will operate as a business consultant, guiding clients toward success while identifying opportunities to expand value and deepen long-term partnerships.
Responsibilities
Serve as the strategic advisor and main point of contact for high-value clients, ensuring the successful delivery of marketing solutions that meet defined KPIs and marketing objectives.
Build, maintain, and grow multi-stakeholder relationships, including executive and senior-level client contacts.
Develop and execute account growth strategies, identifying upsell and cross-sell opportunities that drive incremental revenue.
Monitor account performance through data-driven insights, preparing detailed performance marketing reports and presenting strategic recommendations to clients and leadership.
Lead weekly strategic planning sessions to assess progress, recalibrate priorities, and mitigate risks.
Anticipate client challenges and proactively deliver solutions to maintain satisfaction and retention.
Collaborate with internal stakeholders to optimize workflows, processes, and client delivery standards, ensuring operational excellence.
Represent the agency’s brand and values in all client interactions, maintaining professionalism, transparency, and accountability.
5+ years of experience in digital account management, client services, or business development, preferably within a marketing, advertising, or digital agency.
Exceptional communication, presentation, and stakeholder management skills.
Proficient in discussing paid media strategies across various platforms, including Facebook, Google Ads, Bing, and Demand Generation. Able to engage clients in detailed conversations about campaign performance, targeting, and optimization without the need for additional resources.
Proven track record managing enterprise or high-value accounts with measurable results tied to client growth and retention.
Strong understanding of digital marketing disciplines, including paid media, analytics, SEO, CRM, content, and automation.
Strong analytical skills with the ability to interpret data and use insights to drive strategic decisions.
Proficiency with project management and CRM tools (e.g., ClickUp, HubSpot, Asana, Salesforce).
Must be fluent in English and comfortable communicating with the U.S.-based clients daily.
Schedule
Remote Work
8-hours per day Mon-Fri USA business hours
Salary $ 2000.00

title: Delivery Coordinator (Fixed Term Contract)
description_text:
At peopleworth, we support work where people and performance thrive. As part of our Employer Group, we work with a variety of forward-thinking partners and are excited to share this opportunity that sits within our growing group.
Role Overview
We are seeking a Delivery Coordinator to join our Employer Group for a three-month fixed-term contract. This role will support the delivery of high-quality learning experiences by ensuring smooth coordination across programmes and operational activities. Reporting to the Head of Delivery, this position plays a key part in maintaining exceptional learner support standards and process efficiency across delivery operations.
Key Responsibilities
Coordinate learner onboarding and maintain accurate enrolment and progress records 
Manage student-facing programme information, ensuring clarity and accuracy 
Support delivery processes including extensions, deferrals, assisted study plans, and withdrawals 
Prepare, analyse, and report on learner and performance data to support delivery operations 
Support the Head of Delivery and CX Insights functions with data management and insights generation 
Collaborate with Programme Coordinators, Delivery Managers, and Student Success teams to ensure consistent delivery standards 
Monitor student communities and learning management systems, producing performance and engagement reports 
Coordinate the delivery of key communications and course evaluation processes 
Assist with administrative and operational tasks across the Product Delivery team 
Contribute to the continuous improvement of systems, workflows, and reporting processes 
Proven experience in administrative coordination, preferably within an education or learning delivery environment 
Strong data analysis and reporting skills using spreadsheets and database tools 
Demonstrated ability to manage multiple priorities and deadlines in a fast-paced environment 
Excellent written and verbal communication skills with high attention to detail 
Experience with project management tools, Google Workspace, CRM, and Learning Management Systems preferred 
Strong interpersonal skills and ability to work effectively across diverse teams and stakeholder groups
Demonstrated experience in developing and improving administrative or operational processes 
Collaborative, people-centred performance culture 
Opportunities to grow in a fast-paced environment 
Exposure to innovative education delivery models and operations 
Our Recruitment Process
The peopleworth Employer Group follows a fair, transparent, and multi-stage recruitment process designed to ensure mutual fit.
Application Submission: Complete the online form and answer brief application questions. 
Initial Screening: Your application is reviewed for role alignment; successful candidates move to the longlist. 
Video Interview Stage: You’ll be invited to record short responses to 3–4 role-specific questions. 
Live Interviews: Shortlisted candidates join first-round interviews (and, where applicable, second or third rounds depending on the role). 
Final Shortlist & Verification: Reference and background checks are completed. 
Offer & Contracting: Successful candidates receive formal offers and contract documents. 
Pre-boarding & Onboarding: Once accepted, you’ll complete a pre-boarding process before officially joining your employing organisation within the Employer Group. 
Throughout every stage, we value clear communication, respectful engagement, and timely feedback.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Delivery Coordinator will support the delivery of high-quality learning experiences by coordinating learner onboarding and managing programme information. They will also prepare and analyze learner data and collaborate with various teams to ensure consistent delivery standards.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have proven experience in administrative coordination, preferably in an education environment, along with strong data analysis and reporting skills. Excellent communication skills and the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment are also required.
